context.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

context.goal: Apply a single legal input transition and verify that data_out either remains unchanged or updates in a way consistent with the unknown internal logic.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known inputs: ['clk', 'rst_n', 'input', 'input', 'output']
Drive a one‑cycle high pulse on the 'input' signal while keeping rst_n deasserted.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe data_out on the rising edge of clk following the input pulse; no fixed latency is assumed.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Exercise the extreme logical values on the known input to ensure robust handling at the boundaries of the input domain.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['known inputs']
Apply a sequence of alternating 0 and 1 values on 'input' over several clock cycles.
timing_assumptions: Advance through conservative clocked observations.
Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Monitor data_out after each rising edge of clk; no deterministic latency is required.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.

context.goal: Observe repeated or back-to-back legal operations under conservative timing assumptions.

context.goal: Validate that an active-low reset reliably clears the data_out register and that the module remains stable after reset deassertion.
stimulus_intent: Assert rst_n low for two clock cycles while toggling the 'input' signal, then deassert rst_n and observe data_out.
timing_assumptions: Observe data_out after each rising edge of clk; no fixed latency is assumed.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.

context.goal: Exercise two successive input events with minimal idle cycles to ensure the design handles back‑to‑back activity conservatively.
stimulus_intent: Generate two back‑to‑back pulses on 'input' separated by a single clock cycle.
timing_assumptions: Check data_out after each rising edge of clk; no assumption of exact cycle latency.

generated_files.description: Marks the rendered cocotb test directory as a Python package.

Thin environment wrapper that composes interface, timing-safe waits, and coverage helpers.

Stable runtime helpers exposed to LLM-filled stimulus and oracle TODO blocks.

Rendered edge cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Phase 4 executable Makefile shell; Phase 5 injects run-time variables and executes it.
